---
title: "Aggregate Stability"
---

Aggregate stability methods assess how well soil aggregates resist breakdown when exposed to water or disturbance.

| Item | Description |
|---|---|
| **What it is** | A field or near-field method for assessing the stability of soil aggregates. |
| **Main purpose** | To support understanding of structure and slaking behaviour. |
| **Where used** | Field / near-field |
| **Typical users** | Farmers, advisors, researchers |
| **Typical outputs** | Aggregate stability score or comparative visual result |

## Main target variables

- aggregate stability,
- slaking behaviour,
- structural resistance to breakdown.

## Descriptor groups supported

- structure,
- water behaviour,
- erosion-related interpretation,
- and practical comparison between sites or treatments.
